symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread urticating red-brown lesions (Darier's sign) with flushing and bone pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Red-brown lesions that urticate on friction (Darier's sign) with flushing and marrow-related bone pain are characteristic of mastocytosis.
Skin flushing alone (without airway/CVS/GI features) is not anaphylaxis.
No respiratory/airway involvement argues strongly against current anaphylaxis, though progression must be safety-netted given the history.
No circulatory compromise argues against anaphylactic shock.
Absence of GI involvement further lowers anaphylaxis likelihood.
Diffuse bone pain reflects marrow mast cell infiltrate rather than focal destructive lesions.
Diffuse pain tied to the degranulation timeline is against focal destructive metastatic lesions.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread lesions with positive Darier's sign; patent airway/clear chest (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A positive Darier's sign with a patent airway/clear chest confirms a cutaneous mast cell flare without anaphylaxis in person.
Darier's sign and the chronic urticaria pigmentosa are against a viral exanthem.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known systemic mastocytosis with recurrent anaphylaxis

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established mastocytosis with anaphylaxis history is the substrate and flags a very low escalation threshold.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Widespread urticating lesions and flushing with bone pain but WITHOUT airway, circulatory or GI involvement is a cutaneous/skeletal mast cell flare — not anaphylaxis. It is managed with antihistamine up-dosing, cooling and NSAID avoidance, with a very low threshold to escalate (adrenaline + 000) if any systemic feature appears; the recurrent-anaphylaxis history and living alone lower that threshold further.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anaphylaxis symptom screen (breathing/throat/circulation/GI) — repeat over time
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects progression to anaphylaxis; the key remote safety step.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum tryptase (if seen acutely) and immunology/haematology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To support mastocytosis management; not a remote acute step.
Confirm in-date pens and an ASCIA action plan given recurrent anaphylaxis and living alone.
